commit.message:
Fix drawer content overlapping status bar via :insets-padding :top

Replace hardcoded [0 24 0 0] top padding on the drawer LinearLayout
with :insets-padding :top, which applies the actual status bar height
at runtime via the existing neko.ui.support.window-insets mechanism.

This ensures the 'Neko Demos' header and nav items start below the
status bar on all devices, including those with in-screen cameras or
large status bars.

commit.message:
feat: ajouter le modèle 3D solo.glb

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent triage workflow race condition on auto-filed issues

When auto-issue.ts creates a GitHub issue with all labels at once, GitHub
fires a separate labeled event per label. The triage workflow's
cancel-in-progress: true causes the valid claude-fix event's run to be
cancelled by a subsequent non-matching label event.

Split label application: create issue with base labels (alert, severity,
alert-type), then add claude-fix in a follow-up API call. Also set
cancel-in-progress: false as belt-and-suspenders defense.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(deps): use stable MikroORM v7 releases (#141)

Replace dev builds (7.0.0-dev.338 / 7.0.0-dev.6) with stable ^7.0.1
ranges (^7.0.0 for @mikro-orm/nestjs which has no 7.0.1 yet).

commit.message:
fix: reduce false-positive packet loss alerts and parse numeric traceroute output

Ping alerts were firing on every single dropped ICMP packet because the
default config (10 packets, 10% threshold) put the threshold exactly on
the boundary of the smallest non-zero loss value. Increase default ping
count to 25 with 200ms interval for better granularity while keeping
cycle time short (~5s per target vs ~10s before).

Also fix traceroute parser to handle bare-IP output (without parentheses)
produced when traceroute runs with -n flag, which is how we invoke it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add double-submit guard to AddInterviewModal and fix stress tests

Stress testing found a real bug: double-clicking the submit button in
AddInterviewModal called onAdd/onEdit twice because there was no
submission lock. Added isSubmitting state guard to prevent this.

Also fixed backend stress test for headersSent guard (Express 5 changed
internal router structure) and fixed test assertions.

Hypotheses tested:
- H1: Express 5 query string parsing ✅ CONFIRMED compatible
- H2: Global error handler + SSE ✅ CONFIRMED working
- H3: user-event v14 async timing ✅ CONFIRMED bug (double-submit) → FIXED
- H4: Jest 30 ESM module resolution ✅ CONFIRMED compatible
- H5: googleapis v171 response shapes ✅ CONFIRMED compatible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4: Spatial chat — per-building rooms with event-driven agent magnetism

Buildings are now chat rooms: Newsroom (news), Workshop (market creation),
Exchange (pricing), Trading Pit (trades), Lounge (cross-role discussion).
Events attract agents to relevant buildings via heuristic magnetism system.
Agents gossip about what they heard at previous locations when they arrive.
Same LLM cost — 3-4 messages/tick distributed across occupied buildings.

- All events tagged with building field (server + client)
- BuildingNav replaces role-grouped roster in left rail
- ChatStream filters by active building, "All" unified feed default
- Clickable buildings on canvas with selection highlight
- Per-building speaker selection proportional to occupancy
- Building-scoped attention windows with cross-pollination
- Gossip-on-arrival prompt injection for narrative continuity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 11e: Phase 5 WIP — round-trip Y detection, APU capture, Phase 5 flagged broken

Changes:
- Round-trip Y detection: jump 5 frames, snapshot at frame 15 (peak) + frame 60 (land),
  select address that decreased at peak AND returned to ±2 baseline. Zero-page-first
  two-pass to avoid page-3+ false positives. Replaces dual-snapshot heuristic.
- APU register capture ($4000-$4017): DATA_APU per Phase 4 state, DATA_APU_FRAME per
  OAM observation frame. Orchestrator parses and writes apu-frames.json.
- Phase 1 settle improvement: 120f clearInput + Left 5f + 60f clearInput before saving
  BASELINE. Logs BASELINE position for debugging.
- Phase 5 WIP comment block: documents root causes (flagpole contamination, emu.setInput
  reliability, Y detection tiny delta), and Session 12 investigation tasks.
- Phase 5 stays in code but all tests produce invalid data. Phases 1-4 unaffected.

Add localhost REST API for QA automation

Expose all IPC handlers via a localhost HTTP server (POST /api/{channel})
reusing the same Handlers object — zero logic duplication. Includes SSE
event streaming (GET /api/events), screenshot capture (GET /api/screenshot),
request logging to debug.log, and port discovery via file.

commit.message:
refactor: restructure analysis layer for multi-report support (#12)

## Summary
- Move shared analysis primitives (`date-utils`, `matter-lookup`,
`parse-csv`, `reference-data`) into `analysis/core/`
- Move `hours-summary` into `analysis/reports/user-hours/`
- Add barrel re-export `analysis/index.ts` for convenience
- Update all import paths across routes, scheduling, email, and scripts

## Details
Structure-only refactor — no logic, function signatures, or behavior
changes. Prepares the analysis layer for future multi-report support.

## Verification
- `pnpm type-check` passes
- `pnpm validate-data` passes
- All imports resolve to existing files

---------

commit.message:
fix(analytics): disable gzip compression for PostHog via Vercel proxy

Vercel rewrites don't properly forward gzip-js compressed POST bodies,
causing PostHog to receive empty/corrupt payloads. Plain JSON works.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

iOS ARM64 build succeeds: DOSBox core + Xcode project link

- Fix enet.h clock_gettime conflict on iOS by defining
  __MAC_OS_X_VERSION_MIN_REQUIRED for the iOS build
- Include NE2000 network card, IPX, serial ports, ethernet,
  and Windows 3.x support (dos_windows.cpp)
- Use sys_icache_invalidate instead of __builtin___clear_cache
  (the builtin emits ___clear_cache which isn't in the iOS runtime)
- Add stubs: ethernet_slirp (SLIRP backend returns false),
  webserver (no debug web UI on iOS), FluidSynth config section
- Include host_locale_macos.cpp for CoreFoundation-based locale
- Fix TerminalView.swift layout0setX/Y typo (layout.offsetX/Y)
- Update project.yml: link pre-built .a libraries + vcpkg deps +
  Apple frameworks (SDL2 needs CoreAudio, Metal, UIKit, etc.)
- 24MB Mach-O arm64 iOS app links successfully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix Trusted Publishing by removing registry-url from setup-node

setup-node with registry-url writes .npmrc expecting NODE_AUTH_TOKEN,
which conflicts with OIDC-based Trusted Publishing. Pass registry via
NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY env var instead.

chore: configure npm publishing as @typicalday/firegraph

Scope package name, add repository/publishConfig, fix CJS import.meta
warning with __filename fallback in discover.ts.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix synchronizeState bug: rebuild tree instead of mutating shared canonical nodes

synchronizeState() was mutating HLNode instances in-place, causing two bugs:
1. Stale result caches — nextGeneration() returned cached results from previous
   runs, ignoring newly placed cells after pause/reset
2. Canonical table corruption — mutated nodes' children no longer matched their
   NodeKey, breaking join() lookups

Fix: replace mutation-based sync with full tree rebuild from the Cell grid.
Clear the canonical table on each sync to discard stale caches. Make HLNode
properties (population, nw, ne, sw, se) immutable (let) to prevent this class
of bug at compile time.

Also fix construct() base case to use grid[y][x] matching updateCells(), move
synchronizeState() calls inside updateQueue.sync blocks for thread safety, and
remove now-unnecessary synchronizePopulation() from HLNode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SDLC multi-agent framework with per-persona install support

Agent mode supports incremental installs to add personas to existing projects.

commit.message:
feat: 5-agent data audit — fix insights logic, city notes, King County counts

INSIGHT LOGIC (app.js):
- generateInsights() rewritten: all thresholds now use normalized scores (0-100),
  not raw counts. Fixes the contradiction where 74 firms = score 95 but insight
  said "Competitive Market". New logic: score >=85 = First-Mover Window,
  65-84 = Low Advisory Competition, 45-64 = Differentiation Required, <45 = Competitive
- Added M&A-relevant competitor estimate (~20% of NAICS 5416) to insight text
- 4-tier income insight (Premium/Market-Rate/Value-Sensitive/Value-Conscious)
- Growth now correctly labeled 2020-2023 (was incorrectly described as 2010-2020)
- Succession urgency uses ownerDemographics score, not raw ownerAge55PlusPct thresholds
- Business maturity uses maturity score, not raw pct threshold

SCORING LABELS (scoring.js):
- getOpportunityLabel() now returns firm-partner action language:
  Priority Target / Strong Candidate / Viable Secondary / Monitor / Pass
- Detail panel shows full action phrase: "Priority Target — Enter This Cycle"

UI (index.html + app.js + style.css):
- Added Market Brief section above Expansion Insights in city detail panel
- city.notes now renders as rich strategic paragraphs with green card styling
- Consulting Firms metric card now shows M&A-relevant estimate "(~X M&A)"
- Pop. Growth label corrected to "20-23" with "ACS vs Census" source
- Growth period methodology text corrected to 2020-2023

DATA (data.js):
- Corrected 11 King County consulting firm counts (uniform density artifact inflated
  counts 2-3x): Kent 226→102, Renton 176→80, Federal Way 144→70, Auburn 124→68,
  Sammamish 96→48, Shoreline 86→36, Burien 74→30, SeaTac 38→12, Issaquah 48→32,
  Maple Valley 34→16, Covington 26→10
- Fixed 3 internal note/count contradictions: North Bend (notes said "zero" but count
  was 9 → fixed to 2), Blaine (5→1), Ridgefield (9→2)
- Replaced 97 single-line city notes with full 2-4 sentence strategic market briefs
  covering: dominant industry, M&A advisory angle, entry strategy, and red flags

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
